title: Imbricaria crenulata
taxonomy: Imbricaria crenulata is classified as a species of sea snail, specifically a marine gastropod mollusk. It belongs to the family Mitridae, which comprises a group of large, predatory sea snails. The classification of Imbricaria crenulata is further detailed at the genus and species levels, with the genus Imbricaria being part of the larger family Mitridae. This classification helps to identify and understand the unique characteristics and evolutionary history of Imbricaria crenulata. Understanding the classification of this species is essential for its conservation and management in marine ecosystems.

intro: Imbricaria crenulata, also known as the "Imbricaria tree", is a tropical plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its purported health benefits, including its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and lower blood pressure. The bioactive constituents of Imbricaria crenulata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties.
